content:
# Nested Conditional Statements

## Nested Conditions

The conditional block inside another if/else conditional block is called as *nested conditional block*.
In the below example, *Block 2* is nested conditional block and *condition B* is called nested conditional statement.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/f5dc9588-5ecf-4fa6-bed7-d1683b764b18.PNG)


#### Code

```python
matches_won = int(input())
goals = int(input())
if matches_won > 8:
    if goals > 20:
        print("Hurray")
    print("Winner")
```

#### Input

```
10
22
```

#### Output

```
Hurray
Winner
```

#### Input

```
10
18
```

#### Output

```

Winner
```

## Nested Condition in Else Block


We can also write nested conditions in Else Statement.

In the below example *Block 2* is a nested conditional block.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/421a6a8d-d843-4990-8ec5-d10c0bf7bd9e.PNG)

#### Code

```python
a = 2
b = 3
c = 1
is_a_greatest = (a > b) and (a > c)
if is_a_greatest:
    print(a)
else:
	is_b_greatest = (b > c)
    if is_b_greatest:
        print(b)
    else:
        print(c)
```

#### Output

```

3
```

## Elif Statement


Use the elif statement to have multiple conditional statements between if and else.

The elif statement is optional.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/0fd2445f-711f-493e-9cd1-773e961097eb.PNG)

## Multiple Elif Statements

We can add any number of `elif` statements after `if` conditional block.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/7bca90d3-f681-46c8-8e19-781ab3708338.PNG)


## Execution of Elif Statement

Python will execute the elif block whose expression evaluates to true.  
If multiple `elif` conditions are true, then only the first elif block which is True will be executed.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/c198ad24-ac97-40e8-a999-086acf71eee2.PNG)


## Optional Else Statement

Else statement is not compulsory after `if - elif` statements.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/01e43a8a-99e8-4da4-b159-f7b3d7fbf5ad.PNG)


#### Code

```python
number = 5
is_divisible_by_10 = (number % 10 == 0)
is_divisible_by_5 = (number % 5 == 0)
if is_divisible_by_10:
    print("Divisible by 10")
elif is_divisible_by_5:
    print("Divisible by 5")
else:
   print("Not Divisible by 10 or 5")
```

#### Output

```

Divisible by 5
```

### Possible Mistake

Cannot write an elif statement after `else` statement.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/51acf00f-218d-4439-b635-95603fb8e08d.PNG)
